description_long: This is a wide-mouthed, round ceramic vessel with a bulbous body and a slightly flared rim. The exterior is decorated with hand-painted, zigzagging geometric patterns in earthy red and brown tones, encircling the vessel in two horizontal bands. The surface has a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of traditional pottery. The interior appears unglazed and is a natural terracotta color, suggesting it is designed to hold dry or non-perishable contents. There are no visible handles, lids, or reinforcements, and the vessel's form is simple yet functional. Its size and shape are reminiscent of traditional storage jars or water pots often found in urban or garden settings.
description: A round, wide-mouthed ceramic vessel with red geometric patterns and a matte, earthy finish.
description_view_0: The vessel's front shows the widest part of the body and the clearest view of the zigzag patterns encircling it.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the body and the continuity of the painted bands.
description_view_2: The vessel's profile is visible, showing the flared rim and the even distribution of the decorative motifs.
description_view_3: This angle emphasizes the open mouth and the symmetry of the geometric designs around the vessel.
